---
title: "Conceptual figure — how the joint 4PL delivers z and CTmax in one go"
date: 2026-05-17
author: "Daniel Noble (with Claude assistance)"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    code-fold: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

```{r setup}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)

set.seed(20260517)

# ============================================================================
# Idealised joint-4PL parameters for the teaching figure. Chosen so the
# curves are biologically expressive but the LT50 (relative midpoint) is
# always well above the lower asymptote.
#
# `up`  varies with T: 0.85 at the cold end → 0.65 at the hot end.
#       Kept ≥ 0.65 throughout so the per-curve midpoint never drops
#       below ~0.35 — keeps LT50 above the lower asymptote even at the
#       hottest assay.
# `low` constant at 0.05 (background mortality with all individuals
#       eventually dying).
# `k`   varies with T: 6 at cold end → 10 at hot end (curves get sharper
#       as it gets hotter, a common biological pattern).
# `mid` linear in T with slope −0.15 → relative z = 6.67 °C.
# ============================================================================
# T_bar shifted to 36 (instead of 34) so the synthetic CrIs fan out more
# strongly at the cool extreme and tighten at the hot extreme — matches
# the intuition that more data and tighter LT50 estimates accumulate at
# the hotter assays. All intercept parameters re-anchored so the actual
# curves at the assay temperatures are unchanged.
T_bar    <- 36
ell_0    <- 0.025   # low at T_bar  → 0.10 at T=30, 0.00 at T=38
ell_beta1 <- -0.0125
u_0      <- 0.70    # up at T_bar   → 0.85 at T=30, 0.65 at T=38
u_beta1  <- -0.025
k_0      <- 9       # k at T_bar    → 6 at T=30, 10 at T=38
k_beta1  <- 0.5
beta0    <- 1.2     # mid at T_bar  → 2.10 at T=30, 0.90 at T=38 (log10 min)
beta1    <- -0.15   # mid slope per °C → relative z = 6.67

z_true       <- -1 / beta1                                # 6.667 °C
log10_t_ref  <- log10(60)                                 # 1.778
CTmax_1h_rel <- T_bar + (log10_t_ref - beta0) / beta1     # 32.18 °C

temperatures <- c(30, 32, 34, 36, 38)

# ============================================================================
# Fake posterior draws — multivariate normal around the central parameter
# values, with SDs picked so the resulting uncertainty bands look like the
# Bayesian posterior of a comfortable fit. Used for illustration only.
# ============================================================================
ndraws <- 1000
# SDs on the per-temperature SLOPE parameters (eb1, ub1, kb1, beta1) are
# inflated relative to the intercepts so the CrIs visibly fan out at the
# temperature extremes. Reduced from the previous values so the T=30 CrI
# is still the widest but no longer dominates the figure visually.
post <- tibble(
  ell0_d    = pmin(pmax(rnorm(ndraws, ell_0,    0.010), 0.001), 0.49),
  eb1_d     = rnorm(ndraws, ell_beta1, 0.0045),
  u0_d      = pmin(pmax(rnorm(ndraws, u_0,   0.018), 0.51 ), 0.999),
  ub1_d     = rnorm(ndraws, u_beta1, 0.006),
  k0_d      = exp(rnorm(ndraws, log(k_0), 0.040)),
  kb1_d     = rnorm(ndraws, k_beta1, 0.10),
  beta0_d   = rnorm(ndraws, beta0,   0.040),
  beta1_d   = rnorm(ndraws, beta1,   0.016)
)

# Vectorised helpers — every 4PL sub-parameter is now T-varying.
# Clamps keep the 4PL well-defined (low < 0.5 < up, k > 0) for every
# draw across the extended temperature grid; without these, wider slope
# SDs would push extreme draws into infeasible regions and break the
# absolute-t50 closed form.
low_at <- function(T) pmin(pmax(post$ell0_d + post$eb1_d  * (T - T_bar),
                                0), 0.49)
up_at  <- function(T) pmin(pmax(post$u0_d   + post$ub1_d  * (T - T_bar),
                                0.51), 0.999)
k_at   <- function(T) pmax(post$k0_d   + post$kb1_d  * (T - T_bar), 0.5)
mid_at <- function(T) post$beta0_d + post$beta1_d * (T - T_bar)

# ============================================================================
# Dose-response curves: posterior median + 95% CrI ribbon for each T.
# ============================================================================
log10_t_grid <- seq(-0.3, 3.3, length.out = 250)
curve_post <- expand_grid(temp = temperatures, log10_t = log10_t_grid) |>
  mutate(.row = row_number()) |>
  group_by(.row) |>
  group_modify(~ {
    low_d <- low_at(.x$temp)
    up_d  <- up_at(.x$temp)
    k_d   <- k_at(.x$temp)
    mid_d <- mid_at(.x$temp)
    p_d   <- low_d + (up_d - low_d) / (1 + exp(k_d * (.x$log10_t - mid_d)))
    tibble(p_med = median(p_d),
           p_lo  = quantile(p_d, 0.025, names = FALSE),
           p_hi  = quantile(p_d, 0.975, names = FALSE))
  }) |>
  ungroup() |>
  bind_cols(expand_grid(temp = temperatures, log10_t = log10_t_grid))

# Inflection points at p = (up + low)/2, x = mid(T) — relative t50.
inflections <- tibble(temp = temperatures) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    log10_t50_med = median(mid_at(temp)),
    log10_t50_lo  = quantile(mid_at(temp), 0.025, names = FALSE),
    log10_t50_hi  = quantile(mid_at(temp), 0.975, names = FALSE),
    p_inf_med     = median((up_at(temp) + low_at(temp)) / 2),
    p_inf_lo      = quantile((up_at(temp) + low_at(temp)) / 2, 0.025, names = FALSE),
    p_inf_hi      = quantile((up_at(temp) + low_at(temp)) / 2, 0.975, names = FALSE)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Absolute t50: where each curve crosses p = 0.5.
#   p = low + (up - low) / (1 + exp(k*(log10_t - mid))) = 0.5
#   → log10_t_abs = mid + (1/k) * log((up - 0.5) / (0.5 - low))
abs_t50_at <- function(T) {
  # Use UN-clamped parameters here so the CrI on absolute t50 reflects
  # the full posterior spread at the temperature extremes (the clamped
  # versions used elsewhere would clip many draws to identical boundary
  # values, compressing the CrI). Draws that produce infeasible (up < 0.5
  # or low > 0.5) are returned as NA so downstream quantile / median
  # calls can drop them via na.rm = TRUE.
  low_d <- post$ell0_d + post$eb1_d  * (T - T_bar)
  up_d  <- post$u0_d   + post$ub1_d  * (T - T_bar)
  k_d   <- post$k0_d   + post$kb1_d  * (T - T_bar)
  mid_d <- post$beta0_d + post$beta1_d * (T - T_bar)
  feasible <- (up_d > 0.5) & (low_d < 0.5) & (k_d > 0)
  out <- rep(NA_real_, length(low_d))
  out[feasible] <- mid_d[feasible] +
    (1 / k_d[feasible]) *
      log((up_d[feasible] - 0.5) / (0.5 - low_d[feasible]))
  out
}
abs_crossings <- tibble(temp = temperatures) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    log10_t50_abs_med = median(abs_t50_at(temp), na.rm = TRUE),
    log10_t50_abs_lo  = quantile(abs_t50_at(temp), 0.025,
                                 names = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    log10_t50_abs_hi  = quantile(abs_t50_at(temp), 0.975,
                                 names = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# ============================================================================
# Fitted midpoint trajectory ("secondary slope") — the line in (log10_t, p)
# space that runs through the per-T inflection points, with 95% CrI ribbon
# from the joint posterior.
#
# The line's HORIZONTAL spacing per °C gives relative z directly; the
# vertical drift reflects up(T) shrinking with temperature.
# ============================================================================
# Extend the slope-band temperatures well beyond the assay temperatures so
# the shaded bands run past the first and last dose-response curves and
# don't terminate awkwardly at the data range.
temp_grid_line <- seq(min(temperatures) - 1.5, max(temperatures) + 1.5,
                      length.out = 200)
mid_traj <- tibble(temp = temp_grid_line) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    log10_t50_med = median(mid_at(temp)),
    log10_t50_lo  = quantile(mid_at(temp), 0.025, names = FALSE),
    log10_t50_hi  = quantile(mid_at(temp), 0.975, names = FALSE),
    p_inf_med     = median((up_at(temp) + low_at(temp)) / 2)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Visual band around the relative trajectory: thin horizontal segments
# at many T-values, each representing the 95% CrI on log10_t at that T.
# Overlap blends them into a smooth uniform band along the median line
# (avoids the triangular caps that geom_polygon produces because both x
# and y vary jointly with T).
mid_band_segs <- mid_traj |>
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::row_number() %% 2 == 1)

# Absolute t50 trajectory at p = 0.5 (the line through the open triangles).
abs_traj <- tibble(temp = temp_grid_line) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    log10_t50_abs_med = median(abs_t50_at(temp), na.rm = TRUE),
    log10_t50_abs_lo  = quantile(abs_t50_at(temp), 0.025,
                                 names = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    log10_t50_abs_hi  = quantile(abs_t50_at(temp), 0.975,
                                 names = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup()
# Visual band around the absolute trajectory: same "feathered segments"
# approach as the relative band — every other T in the dense temp grid
# gives a thin horizontal segment from log10_t50_abs_lo to abs_hi at
# p = 0.5. Avoids the polygon artefact (lo/hi closure forming triangles
# at the ends).
abs_band_segs <- abs_traj |>
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::row_number() %% 2 == 1)

# CTmax_1h_rel posterior (where mid(T) = log10(60)).
ctmax_post <- T_bar + (log10_t_ref - post$beta0_d) / post$beta1_d
ctmax_med  <- median(ctmax_post)
ctmax_lo   <- quantile(ctmax_post, 0.025, names = FALSE)
ctmax_hi   <- quantile(ctmax_post, 0.975, names = FALSE)

# CTmax_1h_abs posterior: solve abs_t50_at(T) = log10(60) for T per draw.
# Closed form via linear approximation around the design grid is messy;
# numerically solve per draw using uniroot on a wide T grid is overkill
# for a synthetic posterior. Approximate by fitting a per-draw OLS line
# to (T_grid, abs_t50) and inverting — this is exactly what the truth
# function does in the real simulation driver.
T_grid_inv <- temperatures
abs_per_draw <- vapply(seq_len(ndraws), function(i) {
  abs_vals <- sapply(T_grid_inv, function(T) {
    mid_i <- post$beta0_d[i] + post$beta1_d[i] * (T - T_bar)
    up_i  <- pmin(pmax(post$u0_d[i]  + post$ub1_d[i] * (T - T_bar),
                       0.51), 0.999)
    k_i   <- pmax(post$k0_d[i] + post$kb1_d[i] * (T - T_bar), 0.5)
    low_i <- pmin(pmax(post$ell0_d[i] + post$eb1_d[i] * (T - T_bar),
                       0), 0.49)
    mid_i + (1 / k_i) * log((up_i - 0.5) / pmax(0.5 - low_i, 1e-3))
  })
  fit <- stats::lm(abs_vals ~ T_grid_inv)
  co  <- stats::coef(fit)
  (log10_t_ref - co[[1]]) / co[[2]]
}, numeric(1))
ctmax_abs_med <- median(abs_per_draw)
ctmax_abs_lo  <- quantile(abs_per_draw, 0.025, names = FALSE)
ctmax_abs_hi  <- quantile(abs_per_draw, 0.975, names = FALSE)

# z relative (from mid slope alone).
z_post <- -1 / post$beta1_d
z_med  <- median(z_post)
z_lo   <- quantile(z_post, 0.025, names = FALSE)
z_hi   <- quantile(z_post, 0.975, names = FALSE)

# z absolute (from the per-draw OLS slope of abs_t50 on T).
z_abs_per_draw <- vapply(seq_len(ndraws), function(i) {
  abs_vals <- sapply(T_grid_inv, function(T) {
    mid_i <- post$beta0_d[i] + post$beta1_d[i] * (T - T_bar)
    up_i  <- pmin(pmax(post$u0_d[i]  + post$ub1_d[i] * (T - T_bar),
                       0.51), 0.999)
    k_i   <- pmax(post$k0_d[i] + post$kb1_d[i] * (T - T_bar), 0.5)
    low_i <- pmin(pmax(post$ell0_d[i] + post$eb1_d[i] * (T - T_bar),
                       0), 0.49)
    mid_i + (1 / k_i) * log((up_i - 0.5) / pmax(0.5 - low_i, 1e-3))
  })
  fit <- stats::lm(abs_vals ~ T_grid_inv)
  -1 / stats::coef(fit)[[2]]
}, numeric(1))
z_abs_med <- median(z_abs_per_draw)
z_abs_lo  <- quantile(z_abs_per_draw, 0.025, names = FALSE)
z_abs_hi  <- quantile(z_abs_per_draw, 0.975, names = FALSE)

# Ghost dose-response at CTmax_1h_rel (posterior median): by construction
# its inflection sits on the vertical 60-min reference.
ghost_low <- pmax(ell_0 + ell_beta1 * (ctmax_med - T_bar), 0)
ghost_up  <- u_0 + u_beta1 * (ctmax_med - T_bar)
ghost_k   <- k_0 + k_beta1 * (ctmax_med - T_bar)
ghost_mid <- beta0 + beta1 * (ctmax_med - T_bar)
ghost_p <- tibble(log10_t = log10_t_grid) |>
  mutate(p = ghost_low +
            (ghost_up - ghost_low) /
              (1 + exp(ghost_k * (log10_t - ghost_mid))))

# z bracket anchors — span point 3 (T = 34) to point 5 (T = 38). Same
# slope as any other pair (mid(T) is straight), but anchoring on the
# hotter end keeps the brackets in the busier mid–right area of the
# figure rather than crowding the cool plateaus on the left.
T_lo  <- 34; T_hi <- 38
mid_lo <- beta0 + beta1 * (T_lo - T_bar)
mid_hi <- beta0 + beta1 * (T_hi - T_bar)

# Palette: viridis ordered by temperature.
pal <- viridisLite::viridis(length(temperatures), end = 0.92)

theme_proj <- theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title      = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text       = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title    = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 11)
  )

# ============================================================================
# Single-panel figure.
# ============================================================================
p <- ggplot() +
  # Posterior 95% CrI ribbons around each dose-response curve.
  geom_ribbon(data = curve_post,
              aes(x = log10_t, ymin = p_lo, ymax = p_hi,
                  fill = factor(temp)),
              alpha = 0.18, colour = NA) +
  # Ghost dose-response curve at CTmax_1h_rel. Slimmer line in the same
  # long-dash + deeppink style; no need to compete with the now-removed
  # vertical red connector — the grey 60-min vertical handles that role.
  geom_line(data = ghost_p, aes(log10_t, p),
            colour = "deeppink2", linewidth = 1.0, linetype = "longdash") +
  # Posterior median dose-response curves.
  geom_line(data = curve_post,
            aes(log10_t, p_med, colour = factor(temp), group = temp),
            linewidth = 0.9) +
  # Horizontal reference at p = 0.5 (the absolute-LT50 threshold).
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, colour = "steelblue4",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  # Vertical reference at t = 60 min.
  geom_vline(xintercept = log10_t_ref, colour = "grey55",
             linetype = "dotted") +
  # Relative-t50 trajectory: black median line through the inflection
  # points, with a "feathered" CrI band built from many thin horizontal
  # segments (each = the 95% CrI at one T). Uniform around the line.
  geom_segment(data = mid_band_segs,
               aes(x = log10_t50_lo, xend = log10_t50_hi,
                   y = p_inf_med, yend = p_inf_med),
               colour = "black", alpha = 0.16, linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_line(data = mid_traj,
            aes(log10_t50_med, p_inf_med),
            linewidth = 0.9, colour = "black") +
  # Absolute-t50 trajectory: steel-blue median line at p = 0.5 with a
  # vertical-extent CrI band built from many small rectangles (one per
  # T) centred on p = 0.5. Lighter fill ("steelblue1") at lower alpha
  # so the open triangle markers at the assay temperatures remain
  # clearly visible against the band.
  geom_rect(data = abs_band_segs,
            aes(xmin = log10_t50_abs_lo, xmax = log10_t50_abs_hi,
                ymin = 0.5 - 0.022, ymax = 0.5 + 0.022),
            fill = "steelblue1", alpha = 0.04, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(data = abs_traj,
            aes(log10_t50_abs_med, 0.5),
            linewidth = 0.9, colour = "steelblue4") +
  # Inflection-point dots (relative t50): filled circles.
  geom_errorbarh(data = inflections,
                 aes(y = p_inf_med, xmin = log10_t50_lo, xmax = log10_t50_hi,
                     colour = factor(temp)),
                 height = 0, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = inflections,
             aes(x = log10_t50_med, y = p_inf_med, fill = factor(temp)),
             shape = 21, size = 3.2, stroke = 0.7, colour = "black") +
  # p = 0.5 crossings (absolute t50): open triangles, colored by T.
  geom_errorbarh(data = abs_crossings,
                 aes(y = 0.5,
                     xmin = log10_t50_abs_lo, xmax = log10_t50_abs_hi,
                     colour = factor(temp)),
                 height = 0, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = abs_crossings,
             aes(x = log10_t50_abs_med, y = 0.5, fill = factor(temp)),
             shape = 24, size = 2.8, stroke = 0.7, colour = "steelblue4") +
  # ---- Relative-z bracket between two adjacent inflection dots ----------
  # Y-position of the inflection at each anchor T.
  # rel: bracket sits just below the inflections.
  annotate("segment",
           x = mid_lo, xend = mid_lo,
           y = ((u_0 + u_beta1 * (T_lo - T_bar)) +
                pmax(ell_0 + ell_beta1 * (T_lo - T_bar), 0)) / 2,
           yend = 0.22,
           colour = "black", linetype = "longdash", linewidth = 1.0) +
  annotate("segment",
           x = mid_hi, xend = mid_hi,
           y = ((u_0 + u_beta1 * (T_hi - T_bar)) +
                pmax(ell_0 + ell_beta1 * (T_hi - T_bar), 0)) / 2,
           yend = 0.22,
           colour = "black", linetype = "longdash", linewidth = 1.0) +
  annotate("segment",
           x = mid_hi, xend = mid_lo, y = 0.22, yend = 0.22,
           arrow = arrow(ends = "both", length = unit(0.11, "in")),
           colour = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # ---- Absolute-z bracket between two adjacent p=0.5 crossings ----------
  # abs: bracket flipped ABOVE the p=0.5 line — vertical guides from
  # the triangles go UP to a bracket at y = 0.64, keeping the absolute-z
  # apparatus in the upper half of the plot and the relative-z apparatus
  # in the lower half (clean separation).
  annotate("segment",
           x = abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_lo],
           xend = abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_lo],
           y = 0.5, yend = 0.64,
           colour = "steelblue4", linetype = "longdash", linewidth = 1.0) +
  annotate("segment",
           x = abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_hi],
           xend = abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_hi],
           y = 0.5, yend = 0.64,
           colour = "steelblue4", linetype = "longdash", linewidth = 1.0) +
  annotate("segment",
           x = abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_hi],
           xend = abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_lo],
           y = 0.64, yend = 0.64,
           arrow = arrow(ends = "both", length = unit(0.11, "in")),
           colour = "steelblue4", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # ---- z labels: absolute on top, relative below, text centered in
  # each label (hjust = 0.5).
  annotate("label", x = 0.30, y = 0.82,
           label = sprintf("z (absolute) = %.2f °C\n[%.2f, %.2f]",
                           z_abs_med, z_abs_lo, z_abs_hi),
           hjust = 0.5, colour = "steelblue4", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold",
           fill = "white", label.size = 0, lineheight = 0.95) +
  annotate("curve",
           x = 0.85,
           xend = (
             abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_hi] +
             abs_crossings$log10_t50_abs_med[abs_crossings$temp == T_lo]) / 2 - 0.08,
           y = 0.80, yend = 0.66,
           curvature = -0.20, colour = "steelblue4", linewidth = 0.55,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.08, "in"))) +
  annotate("label", x = 0.30, y = 0.40,
           label = sprintf("z (relative) = %.2f °C\n[%.2f, %.2f]",
                           z_med, z_lo, z_hi),
           hjust = 0.5, colour = "black", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold",
           fill = "white", label.size = 0, lineheight = 0.95) +
  annotate("curve",
           x = 0.85, xend = (mid_lo + mid_hi) / 2 - 0.08,
           y = 0.38, yend = 0.24,
           curvature = -0.22, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.55,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.08, "in"))) +
  # ---- CTmax labels: anchored just left of the 60-min vertical. The
  # grey dotted vline (declared earlier) is the only vertical reference
  # — no second red/pink vertical connector.
  annotate("label", x = log10_t_ref - 0.08, y = 1.09,
           label = sprintf("CTmax 1h (relative) = %.2f °C   [%.2f, %.2f]",
                           ctmax_med, ctmax_lo, ctmax_hi),
           hjust = 1, colour = "deeppink2", size = 3.2,
           fill = "white", label.size = 0) +
  annotate("label", x = log10_t_ref - 0.08, y = 1.04,
           label = sprintf("CTmax 1h (absolute) = %.2f °C   [%.2f, %.2f]",
                           ctmax_abs_med, ctmax_abs_lo, ctmax_abs_hi),
           hjust = 1, colour = "steelblue4", size = 3.2,
           fill = "white", label.size = 0) +
  # t = 60 min label: anchored LEFT of the 60-min vertical, between the
  # upper CrI edge of the T=30 curve (~ y = 0.93) and the bottom of the
  # CTmax-absolute label.
  annotate("text", x = log10_t_ref - 0.05, y = 0.96,
           label = "t = 60 min", hjust = 1, colour = "grey40",
           size  = 4.2, fontface = "italic") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = "Temperature (°C)",
                      aesthetics = c("colour", "fill")) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = log10(c(1, 5, 15, 60, 300, 1500)),
    labels = c("1", "5", "15", "60", "300", "1500")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.13), breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1)) +
  labs(title    = NULL,
       subtitle = NULL,
       x        = "Exposure duration (min, log scale)",
       y        = "Survival probability") +
  theme_proj

# ============================================================================
# Inset — a single 4PL curve with its four parameters labelled, plus the
# equation. Self-contained pedagogical block placed in the top-right of
# the main panel via patchwork::inset_element().
# ============================================================================
in_low   <- 0.10
in_up    <- 0.90
in_k     <- 8
in_mid   <- 1.5
in_grid  <- seq(0.0, 3.0, length.out = 200)
in_curve <- tibble(
  log10_t = in_grid,
  p       = in_low + (in_up - in_low) /
              (1 + exp(in_k * (log10_t - in_mid)))
)

# Tangent at the inflection: slope of p with respect to log10_t at
# log10_t = mid is -k * (up - low) / 4. Drawn as a short segment to
# convey the meaning of k visually.
in_slope <- -in_k * (in_up - in_low) / 4
in_x0    <- in_mid;   in_y0 <- (in_up + in_low) / 2
in_dx    <- 0.18
in_tang  <- tibble(
  x = c(in_x0 - in_dx, in_x0 + in_dx),
  y = c(in_y0 - in_slope * in_dx, in_y0 + in_slope * in_dx)
)

p_inset <- ggplot() +
  # Asymptote reference lines + labels.
  annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 3, y = in_up,  yend = in_up,
           linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 3, y = in_low, yend = in_low,
           linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55") +
  annotate("text", x = 2.95, y = in_up  + 0.05,
           label = "up", hjust = 1, size = 3.6, fontface = "italic") +
  annotate("text", x = 2.95, y = in_low - 0.05,
           label = "low", hjust = 1, size = 3.6, fontface = "italic") +
  # Mid: filled circle at the inflection point (mid, (up+low)/2) — same
  # convention as the inflection dots in the main panel. Vertical dotted
  # guide kept so the eye traces the x-position to the axis.
  annotate("segment", x = in_mid, xend = in_mid, y = 0, yend = in_y0,
           linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55") +
  annotate("point", x = in_mid, y = in_y0,
           shape = 21, size = 3.2, fill = "grey30",
           colour = "black", stroke = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = in_mid + 0.12, y = in_y0,
           label = "mid", hjust = 0, size = 3.6, fontface = "italic") +
  # k (slope at inflection) — tangent line + label.
  geom_line(data = in_tang, aes(x, y),
            linewidth = 0.9, colour = "firebrick") +
  annotate("text", x = in_x0 + in_dx + 0.08, y = in_y0 + in_slope * in_dx + 0.05,
           label = "k", hjust = 0, size = 3.8, fontface = "bold.italic",
           colour = "firebrick") +
  # The 4PL curve itself.
  geom_line(data = in_curve, aes(log10_t, p),
            linewidth = 1.0, colour = "grey15") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 3), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  # Use plain ASCII "log10" to avoid font-render boxes on Unicode subscript.
  labs(x = "log10(t)", y = "p") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.margin      = margin(3, 3, 3, 3)
  )

# Inset: somewhat larger than the tiny corner footprint but still kept
# to the top-right of the main panel so it doesn't crowd the curves.
p_combined <- p + inset_element(p_inset,
                                left = 0.68, bottom = 0.60,
                                right = 0.99, top = 0.99,
                                align_to = "panel")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-derivation
#| fig-width: 8.5
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-cap: "Conceptual figure showing how the joint Bayesian 4PL model delivers both relative and absolute $z$ and $CT_{max,1h}$ from a single dose–response fit. Coloured curves are joint-model posterior medians of survival probability at five assay temperatures (30–38 °C; viridis order); shaded bands are pointwise 95% credible intervals. Top-right inset: the 4PL function with its four parameters labelled."

# Save the publication copy to output/figs/ (SVG, vector-editable).
ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = here::here("output", "figs",
                        "joint_4pl_derivation_conceptual.svg"),
  plot     = p_combined,
  width    = 9.5,
  height   = 6.5,
  units    = "in"
)

p_combined
```
